heroHeadline: Vance Departs for Islamabad as Iran Holds Out; Trump Calls Ceasefire Extension 'Highly Unlikely'
heroSubtitle: Vice President JD Vance, Witkoff, and Kushner departed for Islamabad to lead Round 2 talks, but Iran's Foreign Ministry called the trip 'a media game' and declined to confirm participation, with the ceasefire expiring April 22. Trump declared extension 'highly unlikely' without a deal and reiterated threats to destroy all Iranian power plants and bridges. Brent crude dipped slightly to ~$94.78/bbl on thin optimism; Xi Jinping called Saudi Crown Prince MBS urging Hormuz to stay open. The US Navy forced 21 commercial ships to turn back, and Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf warned Tehran is 'prepared to reveal new cards on the battlefield.'
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-21. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, ABC News, NBC News, PBS NewsHour, Xinhua, Euronews, UN News, Axios, CFR, PRIO, Army Recognition.
